The 2024 Olympics come to an official close Sunday in Paris withthe Closing Ceremony in Stade de Franceto celebrate the ’24 Games and look forward to the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles. The Closing Ceremony is scheduled for 3 p.m. ET (9 p.m. in Paris) withTV coverage on NBCandstreaming on-demand.

2024 OLYMPIC GAMES

Closing Ceremony at a glance

When:Sunday, Aug. 11 at 3 p.m. ET (9 p.m. local)

Where:Stade de France, Paris, France

TV channel:NBC

Live streams:FuboTV (free trial)|SlingTV (low intro rate)|DirecTV Stream (free trial)|Peacock ($7.99+ per month)

Whispers of surprises are circulating about potential musical acts and entertainers who could perform at the 2024 Olympics Closing Ceremony, and the Paris 2024 Olympic Committee announced “over a hundred” performers will take part in it. Thomas Jolly, who planned the Opening Ceremony that caused a social media stir in the U.S., also planned the closing . Swimmer Katie Ledecky and rower Nick Mead will be flag bearers for the USA.

On the TV side, NBC’s Mike Tirico will host the Closing Ceremony alongside Jimmy Fallon, Terry Gannon, Tara Lipinski and Johnny Weir. The ceremony in Paris will also feature the city handover from Paris to Los Angeles, which will host the 2028 Olympics.

SEE ALSO: Team USA gear:Fanatics|Ralph Lauren|Walmart|USA Basketball gear

Olympics TV and streaming:More on Peacock’s Gold Zone|Alex Cooper to host interactive watch parties

The 2024 Olympics Closing Ceremony is set for a 3 p.m. ET start on NBC. Live streams are available fromFuboTV (free trial),SlingTV (low intro rate)andDirecTV Stream (free trial).
